lyrics:
He moves through the blue light
Like a signal through the wall
No name on his lips now
No truth at all

His shadow in the doorway
His hand against the glass
He leaves the room electric
Then he passes

I wait where his silence bends
I break where his current ends

Every time he reappears
I’m lost within his wires
Every time he disappears
It’s just a waste of tears

A shape inside the static
A face I almost know
He pulls me like a circuit
Then lets go

No promise in his movement
No mercy in his glow
I feel him in the distance
Cold and close

I wait where his silence bends
I break where his current ends

Every time he reappears
I’m lost within his wires
Every time he disappears
It’s just a waste of tears

Under skin, under sleep
He is not mine to keep
In the hum, in the dark
He leaves his mark

Call it love
Call it fear
Still I know
He was here

Every time he reappears
I’m lost within his wires
Every time he disappears
It’s just a waste of tears

Every time he reappears
I’m drawn back to the fire
Every time he disappears
It’s just a waste of tears

Just a waste of tears
Just a waste of tears
persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This high-energy electronic track creates a tense, futuristic atmosphere by contrasting a driving, four-on-the-floor beat with breathy, distant female vocals. The production is clean and powerful, featuring a deep, punchy kick and bass that sit tightly in the low end while crisp, arpeggiated synths and spacious vocal effects fill out the wide stereo image. The song's structure effectively builds and releases tension, moving from sparser, more rhythmic verses into an anthemic instrumental chorus. A defining quality is the sharp, aggressive synth bass that often doubles the main rhythmic pulse, giving the track a relentless and edgy feel.

review.review_text: At 129 BPM in F minor, 'Just a Waste of Tears' runs colder and more controlled than anything STAB has put out recently, and that restraint is exactly what makes it land. The chorus hook, 'I'm lost within his wires / every time he disappears,' earns its repetition by the third time around, sitting in that space where emotional resignation and melodic insistence pull in opposite directions. The production keeps things clean and pressurized, a punchy low end holding the verses taut while the song opens up just enough on the chorus to let the grief breathe. This is STAB leaning into her more introspective side, and she sounds completely at home there.
anchors_used: chorus lyric 'I'm lost within his wires / every time he disappears'
